title: add_group_member_capped returns Ok(true) for already-a-member, hiding the idempotent case
description: store.rs add_group_member_capped() returns Ok(true) both when 'member was added' and when 'member was already present' (INSERT OR IGNORE). The caller cannot distinguish these two cases. The GROUP_ADD handler sends a GROUP_DELIVER notification to the new member unconditionally — if the member was already in the group, they receive a spurious 'you were added' notification. Should return Ok(AddResult::Added | AlreadyMember | CapReached) to let the caller suppress the redundant notification.
